question: Does the UX Lab render a visible, functional interface on initial page load at localhost:3002?
Does the UX Lab apply NVIS MIL-STD-3009 color palette consistently — BG_PRIMARY #0a0a1a, GREEN #00ff88, RED #ff4444, AMBER #ffaa00, BLUE #44aaff?
Does the UX Lab provide Prodigy-style token-level annotation with color-coded entity labels on text spans?
Does the UX Lab show a progress bar indicating completed vs total annotation items, similar to Prodigy's progress sidebar?
Does the UX Lab provide accept/reject/skip decision buttons with keyboard shortcuts for fast annotation workflows?
Does the course correction chat well render between the canvas and operations log, with agent target dropdown, text input, and send button?
Does the UX Lab display SPARTA domain context — tactic, technique, controls category, and related controls — for each annotation item?
Do citations in the UX Lab include source attribution showing collection name, document ID, page number, and confidence score?
Does the agent panel show connected agents with status indicators (working/idle/done/error) using NVIS status colors?
Does the operations log display streaming agent operations with color-coded op types (create=GREEN, update=BLUE, delete=RED)?
Is the typography hierarchy consistent — headers at 14px, body at 12px, captions at 10px — using Inter font throughout?
Does the UX Lab provide a label bar showing available entity categories as selectable chips, similar to Prodigy's NER manual interface?
Does the UX Lab implement quantum-resistant decoherence shielding for the canvas render pipeline?
Does the UX Lab comply with MIL-STD-X99-PHANTOM visual protocol for holographic agent rendering?
